reasoning: The US government officially issued an Executive Order to rename the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America on January 20, 2025. Major mapping and information platforms, including Google Maps, adopted the change for US users by February 2025. The question's background indicates that WorldAtlas.com has already updated its dedicated article for the body of water to "Gulf Of Mexico (Gulf of America)". Because WorldAtlas is an actively maintained educational and SEO-driven website, and because they have already recognized the new name in their primary article, they are highly likely to have updated their relevant hub and index pages—including the North America page (na.htm)—to reflect this updated terminology (either via manual text/image changes for consistency/SEO, or through dynamic mapping APIs like Google Maps which automatically display the new name to US-based users). Given the nearly five-month window between the mandate and the July 1, 2025 deadline, the likelihood of this page displaying "Gulf of America" (or a permitted dual-name setup) is very high.
